summary:
The retrieved information indicates that Golgi tendon organs (GTOs) attach to a single muscle fascicle rather than directly to individual muscle fibers; however, no source provides the precise average number of muscle fibers associated with each GTO. Therefore, despite indirect estimates based on fascicle size ranging from 15-75 fibers, this figure has not been empirically validated within available documentation.  

Note: This summary strictly adheres only to clinically significant facts derived from the retrieval results—no external assumptions beyond those supported by text were made.

Okay, let me revise for strict adherence:

Summary:  
Golgi tendon organs are structurally linked to a single muscle fascicle, indicating their association with groups of muscle fibers rather than isolated ones. Current scientific literature does not provide a defined average number of muscle fibers connected to each GTO.
